description_long: This object consists of three wooden barrels grouped together on a flat, hexagonal base. Each barrel is constructed from vertical wooden staves, bound by two metallic hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The barrels have polygonal, slightly faceted tops and bottoms, giving them a stylized, low-poly appearance. The wood texture is medium brown with visible grain, while the metal bands are a dull silver-gray. The barrels appear empty and are likely hollow inside, as is typical for such containers. The overall look suggests these barrels are used for storage or transport of goods in an urban or industrial setting.
description: A group of three stylized wooden barrels with metal bands, arranged on a hexagonal base. The barrels have faceted tops and a medium brown wood texture.
description_view_0: Two barrels are most visible, with the third partially obscured behind them; the metal bands and wood grain are clearly seen.
description_view_1: All three barrels are visible, with the central barrel most prominent and the others flanking it on either side.
description_view_2: The two barrels in the foreground are fully visible, with the third barrel mostly hidden behind them.
description_view_3: The three barrels are evenly spaced and all are clearly visible, showing the symmetry of their arrangement.
